title: Additional Field
description: A merchant-defined buyer-input field surfaced on a checkout or cart response. Represents extra information the business requests (e.g., consent, delivery window, delivery instructions, gift message). Platforms submit only `{key, value}` entries; the business is authoritative for validation and persistence.

key.description: Stable, human-readable field identifier, unique within a single checkout or cart response. Format is business-defined but SHOULD use a dot-separated namespaced form to aid debugging and prevent collisions (e.g., `custom.delivery_instructions`).

label.description: Resolved localized display label for the field. Plain text; platforms MUST NOT interpret formatting sequences (e.g., markdown, HTML).

description.description: Resolved localized help text for the field. Interpreted according to `description_content_type`: plain text by default, or link-only markdown when `description_content_type` is `markdown`.

description_content_type.description: Content format for `description`. Defaults to `plain`. When `markdown`, platforms MAY render links only, MUST reject or strip all other markdown constructs and raw HTML, MUST require HTTPS URLs, and MAY fall back to plain-text rendering.

required.description: When true, the buyer MUST submit a non-null `value` before checkout can complete, subject to type-specific emptiness rules. The business is authoritative for this flag; platforms use it as a rendering and submission hint.

value.description: Current submitted value, or `null` when unset. The JSON type of `value` matches the field's `input.type`: `boolean` fields use JSON booleans (`true` / `false`); `date` fields use RFC 3339 full-date strings (`YYYY-MM-DD`); `choice` fields use one of the strings in `input.options[].value`; `text` and `multiline_text` fields use raw strings. Platforms submit `{key, value}` pairs to set the value.

input.description: Typed descriptor for the kind of value this field accepts, plus optional validation hints.
